'''Vince Fawcett''' (13 November 1970 – 6 February 2026) was an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Workington Town]], the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham Bears]], the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], as a {{rlp|wg|so}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Vince Fawcett was born in [[Leeds]], West Riding of Yorkshire, England on 13 November 1970.

==Playing career==
Fawcett struggled to fulfil his potential in a star-studded but underachieving [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] side, but thrived in a big-fish-in-a-small-pond role at [[Workington Town]] in the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95]] season when Workington Town, back in the game's top division, secured a mid-table finish. Fawcett enjoyed an excellent season and scored three tries against his old side.

His form alerted [[Parramatta Eels]] where he enjoyed a successful short stint in 1995 where he is best remembered for a match-winning performance against arch rivals Canterbury-Bankstown, as his two tries helped Parramatta register a shock win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

The big {{rlp|ce}} returned to England with [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham Bears]] in 1997, scoring only three tries, but they all came in the same game against [[Challenge Cup]] holders, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Fawcett went on to play [[Super League]] for [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

==Death==
Fawcett died on 9 February 2026, at the age of 55.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 February 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Leeds, Warrington and Wakefield man Vince Fawcett dies aged 55|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/vince-fawcett-dies-aged-55}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Former Warrington Wolves player dies after long illness |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/25837095.former-warrington-wolves-player-dies-long-illness/ |access-date=13 February 2026 |publisher=Warrington Guardian |date=9 February 2026}}</ref>

==International honours==
Fawcett appeared for Great Britain at under-21 level. He was named in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 40-man training squad in preparation for the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Larder leaves out Schofield |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-larder-leaves-out-schofield-1592364.html |website=The Independent |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=19 July 1995}}</ref> but was not included in the final squad.
